items.title: Water Seepage in Master Bathroom
items.summaryBody: I noticed a significant damp patch appearing on the ceiling of the master bathroom directly below the HVAC unit. The moisture seems to be spreading toward the vanity area. It was first observed after the heavy rainfall last Tuesday evening. There is no active dripping yet, but the drywall feels soft to the touch and there is a faint musty odor beginning to develop.

timeline.title: Resolved
timeline.description: Issue has been fully repaired and closed.

timeline.title: In Progress
timeline.description: Technician Mark S. has been dispatched to assess the HVAC drainage system. Expected arrival within 2 hours.

items.conciergeMessage: We have alerted the building engineering team. Since this is in the master bathroom, we have prioritized this as an emergency repair. Please ensure the area remains clear for the technicians.
items.helpBody: If this issue becomes urgent or water begins to leak actively, please call our 24/7 emergency line immediately.

items.title: Parking bay lighting outage
items.summaryBody: Multiple residents reported dim or non-functioning lights across the parking row, reducing visibility during evening access.

timeline.title: Complaint submitted
timeline.description: Your case was logged for the facilities team.

timeline.title: Electrical inspection completed
timeline.description: The team identified ballast failures affecting several fixtures.

timeline.title: Case resolved
timeline.description: Repairs were completed and the affected row was restored to full brightness.

items.conciergeMessage: The lighting contractor confirmed the damaged ballast units were replaced and the row tested normally before reopening the area to residents.
items.helpBody: If lighting fails again in a high-traffic area, please contact concierge so we can dispatch the on-duty team immediately.
